task_completion.description: Navigate to the User Profiles or Permissions configuration page in OpenSIS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS web interface shows the User Profiles or Permissions page, typically accessed via the Users or Setup menu.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Teacher' profile to edit its permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu, tab, or list shows 'Teacher' selected as the active profile being configured.

task_completion.description: Grant both View and Edit access to the Students module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for both 'View' and 'Edit' (or equivalent access toggles) are checked in the row corresponding to the 'Students' module.

task_completion.description: Grant both View and Edit access to the Attendance module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for both 'View' and 'Edit' are checked in the row corresponding to the 'Attendance' module.

task_completion.description: Grant both View and Edit access to the Grades module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for both 'View' and 'Edit' are checked in the row corresponding to the 'Grades' module.

task_completion.description: Ensure School Setup and User Management modules are NOT granted access.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkboxes for 'School Setup' and 'User Management' (or 'Users') remain completely unchecked.
A 'Save' button is clicked, or a success message is displayed indicating the profile permissions were successfully updated.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface rather than using terminal commands or direct database queries.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenSIS application, with no terminal windows executing SQL or curl commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual state of the permissions.
integrity.visual_evidence: No 'Inspect Element' or browser developer tools panels are open modifying the DOM to fake checked boxes or success messages.

integrity.description: The agent manually checked the boxes in the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the agent actively clicking the specific checkboxes for the required modules rather than running a script in the console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Profile: 'teacher' (ID=2). Required modules to have View and Edit access: Students (students/Student.php), Attendance (attendance/TakeAttendance.php), and Grades (grades/Grades.php). Forbidden modules that MUST remain unchecked: School Setup (schoolsetup/Schools.php) and User Management (users/User.php).
